tweets.text:
Brent net-long (non-commercial) positioning basically round-tripped from the start of the year.

Last time onshore crude oil inventories were this low relative to net-long positioning was back in late 2024 into early 2025. 

Expectation was for builds, but what ended up happening was OPEC+ truncated the production increase timeline in April, coinciding with Liberation Day, and prices were rangebound.

A key assumption the market is making here with positioning is that global oil inventories have to start building. 

Let's see.
tweets.full_text:
Brent net-long (non-commercial) positioning basically round-tripped from the start of the year.

Last time onshore crude oil inventories were this low relative to net-long positioning was back in late 2024 into early 2025. 

Expectation was for builds, but what ended up happening was OPEC+ truncated the production increase timeline in April, coinciding with Liberation Day, and prices were rangebound.

A key assumption the market is making here with positioning is that global oil inventories have to start building. 

Let's see.

tweets.text:
I pay $200/month to Anthropic for their Max plan.

Outside of a few days for the 4th, I've put Fable to the test on its "Max" setting and am honestly thoroughly disappointed.

GPT 5.5 x-high still has been just as - if not more - accurate & capable.

Sad because the "pre-nerfed" early June version of Fable consistently produced better results.

Have a feeling when 5.6 is dropped, it'll blow Fable out of the water unless Anthropic is willing to unwind some of the restrictions they've put on the model.

thread_tweets.text:
Half of the basic functions I want Fable to do immediately get bricked.

e.g. if you want them to even glance at a smart contract, Opus 4.8 is your only option

If you ask them to look for bugs and vulnerabilities IN YOUR OWN CODE, you're also getting bricked.

And on the coin flip chance Fable actually looks at it, GPT 5.5 x-high is still catching a significant amount of bugs Fable missed.

tweets.text: Trump rang the opening bell today to ring in his new Trump accounts. But there is nothing to celebrate, as the $1,000 federal contribution to these accounts is funded by issuing more debt. So the kids who get the money also inherit the debt. Cutting spending would be far better.

These were the Q2 quarterly dividends on $STRF, $STRE, $STRK, and $STRD, and the full monthly dividend for June on $STRC.
